title: eriogonum longifolium var. longifolium
intro: Eriogonum longifolium var. longifolium is a perennial shrub native to the western United States and Canada, belonging to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae), and is also known as long-leaved buckwheat or Mormon tea. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum longifolium var. longifolium include relief from colds, coughs, and sore throats, as well as its use in treating kidney stones and other urinary issues. Eriogonum longifolium var. longifolium can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used as a poultice to treat skin irritations and wounds.
